articles.title: Stripe in Talks to Buy Buzzy AI-Model Marketplace OpenRouter
articles.summary: Stripe is in talks to acquire OpenRouter, a startup that helps developers choose between AI models. The talks are still ongoing and could fall apart. Industry experts estimate that the business could fetch about $10 billion in a sale. Stripe is separately pursuing a deal for PayPal.

articles.title: OpenAI makes ChatGPT Health available to all US users
articles.summary: ChatGPT Health is now available to all US-based users over 18. The feature helps users with health-related queries. Users can let the feature access data from apps and integrate medical records from hospital systems. While several studies have found that AI bots are not reliable for medical advice, this has not deterred AI companies from launching health-related features.

articles.title: The Robots Cometh
articles.summary: Unitree's GD01 is a transformable mecha robot. It can transport a pilot in its torso-mounted cockpit, walk on either two legs or four, and wield fists powerful enough to knock down walls. Standing 9 ft tall and weighing half a ton, it has heavy-duty mechanical hinges that connect titanium-alloy limbs to a protective carbon fiber casing. Pictures of the robot are available in the article.

articles.title: SpaceX Is Turning Away Falcon Customers in Major Bet on Starship
articles.summary: SpaceX has started to turn away satellite operators seeking dedicated rides on its Falcon 9 rocket beyond 2028. The company has also stopped building some non-reusable components for the Falcon family. SpaceX has publicly stated that it intends to offramp its Falcon 9 rocket for Starship, but has not disclosed a timeline. The company is likely to still use the Falcon 9 for launches for the Department of Defense and NASA.

articles.title: Engineer away the slop
articles.summary: The field of formal verification and deterministic system testing is about to cross a chasm. There are not enough skilled practitioners in the field. Bug-catching tools, when used in conjunction with adversarial code reviews by an LLM and with language analyzers driven by pre-commit hooks, will be a key component in software factories. They will enable people/agents to deliver reliable software without the burden of having to learn this specialized knowledge.

articles.title: Why Software Factories Fail
articles.summary: No amount of harness engineering or loopsmaxxing can solve what is fundamentally a model-training issue. Engineers are still stuck reading code. Companies can move 10x-100x faster by convincing themselves that the code doesn't matter anymore, but they could also embrace their constraints and just move 2x-3x faster, safely.

articles.title: Frontier Diffusion & Control
articles.summary: Optimizing the cost-to-outcome frontier in real world context means using the right model for each task, and optimizing the context, skills, tools, and agent harness around it. It's possible to take saturated frontier capabilities and deliver them at scale and at lower cost through models optimized for high-usage products, while continuing to use frontier models for frontier needs. The model is only one part of the hill-climbing system. Harness, memory, context, tools, skills, and user interactions all shape the evals and performance of these agentic systems.

articles.title: Inside China's All-Out Push to Catch Up With American AI Chips
articles.summary: Huawei is leading the way in reducing China's dependence on foreign AI chips. The company claims it has figured out workarounds for making near state-of-the-art silicon without leading-edge machinery. Huawei's deputy chairman attributes the company's progress to the restrictions the US has imposed on hardware exports. China still lags far behind the US, and chip-production capacity problems are preventing Chinese companies from deploying AI as quickly as they would like.

articles.title: Canvas UI (GitHub Repo)
articles.summary: Canvas UI is an open source library of creative, framework-agnostic components drawn on canvas.

articles.title: Frontier
articles.summary: The 'frontier' moniker justifies capital spending to investors, who believe that the spending is the moat.

articles.title: Google Study Says AI Is Helping Workers, Not Replacing Them
articles.summary: Google's research points to a future where the demand for highly skilled workers is accentuated rather than diminished.

articles.title: Everyone Should Know SIMD
articles.summary: SIMD is just about as easy as a for loop.

articles.summary: Download a dictation app and start talking.

articles.title: Forgot your Google password? Now you can log in with a selfie
articles.summary: Google has added a new video selfie option for account recovery.
